title: Ones
description:
Implement `ones(i)`, which returns a length-$i$ vector of $1$s:

$$\text{ones}(i)_k = 1 \quad \text{for } k = 0, \dots, i-1$$

**Input**: `i` (python int, the length). **Output**: a 1-D tensor of shape `[i]` filled with ones.

You may use `torch.arange` and comparisons; broadcasting only. `torch.ones`/`torch.zeros` are banned so you must build the vector another way.
starter_code:
import torch

def ones(i):
    # Return a length-i vector of 1s, without calling torch.ones/torch.zeros.
    pass
hints: Solve in 1 line using broadcasting only.
torch.arange(i) is always >= 0 — a comparison against arange gives you a boolean vector you can cast to get 1s.

solutions.name: arange + comparison
solutions.explanation: `torch.arange(i)` produces $[0, 1, \dots, i-1]$, all of which are $\geq 0$. Comparing gives an all-`True` boolean vector; casting to an integer dtype turns every `True` into a `1`.
